summary: hipEngine resident c=1 now matches parent router/shared-gate BF16 materialization and parent q/k head-norm offset preparation; prefill seed is restored to 4403, but first decode token still mismatches parent 1739 vs HIP 4096.

commands.parent_topk_probe: cd /home/lhl/amd-gpu-tuning && <OPTIMAL env flags> PYTHONPATH=nano-vllm-amd:paroquant mamba run -n therock --no-capture-output python3 /tmp/hipengine_parent_paro_topk_probe.py
commands.targeted_tests: python3 -m compileall -q hipengine tests scripts && python3 -m pytest tests/test_qwen35_paro_layout.py tests/test_loading_materialize.py tests/test_qwen35_decode_state.py -q

fixes_validated: runtime-prepared router_shared_gate.weight now materializes as BF16 bits, matching parent native router_shared_weight
full-attention q_norm/k_norm now materialize parent fused-kernel BF16 offsets as (checkpoint+1 -> FP16 -> BF16 -> -1), instead of checkpoint-direct BF16
HIP prefill seed returned to 4403 after the router BF16 fix
blockers: First decode token remains wrong after router/qk-norm materialization fixes: parent sequential and bulk first decode argmax 1739; HIP argmax 4096.

Forced parent seed 4403 before the router fix produced HIP token 329, proving the previous seed-220 state was invalid; after the router fix, the remaining blocker is a close top-logit ordering/state drift, not the broad router dtype bug.
Next evidence should bisect per-layer hidden/logit outputs against parent sequential mode to find where HIP raises 4096 and 220 relative to 1739.
